item.title: Arif Ahmed made Mongla Port chairman
item.description: Rear Admiral Arif Ahmed Mustafa assumed charge as new chairman of Mongla Port Authority on Sunday....

item.title: Intern doctors go on strike
item.description: Intern doctors across the public and private medical college hospitals on Sunday began an indefinite work abstention, pressing for a six-point demand, including increase in allowances and the cancellation of the health ministry’s decision on FCPS training....

item.title: Daffodil Polytechnic signs memo with Sias University in China
item.description: The Daffodil Polytechnic Institute has signed a memorandum of understanding with the Sias University in China recently....

item.title: 12 wounded in Ohio shooting
item.description: A shootout Saturday near a community festival in Toledo, Ohio left at least 12 people wounded by gunfire, with no suspects in custody, police said....

item.title: Elderly man beaten to death
item.description: An elderly man was allegedly beaten to death by rivals over ownership disputes centring a piece of land at Manda upazila in Naogaon on Sunday....

item.title: Russian strikes kill 5, damage nuclear facility in Ukraine
item.description: Russia fired waves of drones and other munitions at Ukraine on Sunday, killing at least five people and damaging a nuclear storage facility in the Chernobyl exclusion zone, Ukrainian officials said....

item.title: ISD students secures $1.7m scholarships at univs worldwide
item.description: International School Dhaka’s graduating class of 2026 has secured international university scholarships worth $1.7 million....

item.title: Frequent power cuts hit N’ganj
item.description: A severe power crisis hit Narayanganj city and surrounding areas on Sunday after a snapped cable at a grid substation disrupted power transmission, triggering frequent power cuts throughout the day....

item.title: Inflation increases two months in a row
item.description: The general inflation rose to 9.42 per cent in May, marking the increase of commodities prices for two consecutive months......

item.title: War on Iran reaches 100th day with no end in sight
item.description: The United States said it shot down a pair of Iranian drones threatening the Strait of Hormuz, as the Middle East war reached its 100th day on Sunday with no end in sight....

item.title: Fiscal discrimination barrier to green energy transition: CPD
item.description: The Centre for Policy Dialogue on Sunday urged the government to end what it termed ‘fiscal discrimination’ in favour of fossil fuels and adopt a comprehensive green fiscal policy to facilitate Bangladesh’s transition to renewable energy and address the ongoing energy crisis......

item.title: Unrest triggers Tk 3,500cr withdrawal from IBBL
item.description: Continuous unrest at Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC has triggered fresh concern among depositors, leading to net withdrawals of about Tk 3,500 crore over the past five days amid fears about the bank’s stability......

item.title: Magnitude 5.6 earthquake in Bhutan felt in Bangladesh capital
item.description: Tremors from a magnitude 5.6 earthquake that struck western Bhutan on Sunday were felt in Dhaka and other parts of Bangladesh, according to residents....

item.title: Israel strikes south Beirut
item.description: Israel’s military struck Beirut’s southern suburbs on Sunday, hitting apartments in two buildings, after saying it had intercepted projectiles launched by Hezbollah into Israeli territory....

item.title: Remittance from UAE, Qatar, Bahrain plunges amid ME crisis
item.description: Remittance inflows from three key Gulf countries — the United Arab Emirates, Qatar and Bahrain — fell sharply in the January–March quarter of fiscal year 2025-26 amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East......

item.title: Banks’ reform to be brought under commission, says Swapon
item.description: The government would bring the banking sector under a reform commission framework as comprehensive restructuring of the troubled sector had become unavoidable amid growing concerns over governance failures, loan scams and depositor suffering......

item.title: NBR extends deadline for e-VAT return
item.description: The National Board of Revenue has extended the deadline for businesses to enter previously submitted paper-based VAT returns into the electronic VAT or e-VAT system until June 30 of this year, ahead of the planned introduction of mandatory online VAT return filing from July......

item.title: Pay hike, polls pledges puff up new budget
item.description: The national budget for the financial year 2026-27 is set to get bigger by Tk8,000 crore mainly to implement the new pay scale for its employees as well as meeting its electoral pledges and payment of subsidies, officials have said....

item.title: JS allocates 40hrs for budget debate, session to end on July 9
item.description: The third meeting of the Business Advisory Committee of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday allocated 40 hours for discussions on the national budget for the 2026-27 financial year....

item.title: REHAB seeks special fund, single-digit interest loans
item.description: A delegation from the Real Estate and Housing Association of Bangladesh held a courtesy meeting with Bangladesh Bank governor Md Mostaqur Rahman on Sunday at the central bank premises......

item.title: MPs censure govt over power cuts
item.description: Power and energy minister Iqbal Hassan Mahmood on Sunday faced a barrage of questions from lawmakers over the ongoing loadshedding across the country on the opening day of the second session of the Jatiya Sangsad....

item.title: Luxury hotels in Dubai woo staycationers as tourists flee
item.description: Once reserved for wealthy travellers, Dubai’s top-notch hotels have become almost exclusively reliant on residents, drawn in by dizzying staycation offers as war drives tourists away......

item.title: BGB-BSF summit begins amid Indian push-in bids
item.description: The 57th four-day director general-level talks between the Border Guard Bangladesh and the Indian Border Security Force begins today in India’s capital New Delhi amid a face-off between the two forces, especially over pushing people into Bangladesh....

item.title: More traffic, halved profits for airlines in 2026: IATA
item.description: Airlines are expecting higher passenger numbers in 2026 but half the profits of last year, according to forecasts released on Sunday at a conference in Brazil, as rising fuel costs fail to deter travellers......

item.title: Power sector needs review of deals, not tariff increase
item.description: THE Finance Division is reported to be setting aside Tk 370 billion in the forthcoming budget in power-sector subsidy. Yet, the Power Development Board could face a gap of about Tk 150 billion. But what it indicates is a possible power price increase as much to the detriment of consumers as to the benefit of private power producers, who have for years been a drain on the......

item.title: Govt response to measles outbreak falls short
item.description: THE outbreak of measles, with 63,134 hospital admissions and 612 deaths since March, is not merely a public health emergency but also a growing economic crisis for thousands of families. As hospitals struggle to cope with the influx of patients, the burden of treatment is falling heavily on households, particularly those with limited incomes. Parents are selling assets......

item.title: Moving beyond ‘unpeopling’
item.description: AN ANALYSIS of Bangladesh’s national budget since independence shows that marginalised groups such as family farmers, rural women, indigenous peoples and those targeted for agricultural-land-water-body reforms have consistently remained neglected and deprived. For these groups, no effective ‘line item’-based allocations have ever been established in the......

item.title: Centralised agent information system
item.description: AGENT banking has emerged as a powerful driver of financial inclusion which is run by a guideline published in 2017 by the central bank. Introduced as an innovative extension of traditional banking, this model enables commercial banks to provide essential financial services to remote populations through third-party agents. Over the last decade, agent banking......

item.title: Bangladesh’s bid for strategic independence
item.description: THE timing was deliberate. Turkish foreign minister Hakan Fidan arrived in Bangladesh this week as part of a broader South Asia tour that also included Singapore, Indonesia, and South Korea — a regional sweep that underscored how seriously Ankara is now treating its eastern flank. Fidan’s two-day official visit to Dhaka, which began on June 4, comes as Bangladesh......

item.title: From Tokyo’s lost decade to Dhaka’s crossroads
item.description: A WALK through Tokyo’s Akihabara on any weekday afternoon will bring up something quiet, almost unsettling, beneath the neon glare: prices on coffee, ramen, manga and even electronics have barely budged in years. A walk through Dhaka’s New Market the same afternoon brings up an inverse picture. Vegetable prices climb between morning and evening......

item.title: Zverev ends wait for Grand Slam title with French Open triumph
item.description:
Alexander Zverev finally secured his maiden Grand Slam title with a dramatic five-set victory over Italy's Flavio Cobolli in the French Open final on Sunday.
...

item.title: Denmark's Eriksen collapses during Ukraine friendly
item.description:
Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine on Sunday, five years after suffering a cardiac arrest during the European Championships.
...

item.title: Iranian missiles target Israel, says Israeli army
item.description: ‘A short while ago, sirens were sounded in several areas across the country, following the identification of missiles launched from Iran toward the State of Israel,’ the military said…...

item.title: Narayanganj teen dies four days after being stabbed by muggers
item.description: A teenager who was critically injured in stabbing by muggers in the river port area in Narayanganj died on Sunday, four days after the attack....

item.title: Second derailment at same spot within 17 days triggers concern
item.description: Rail communication on between Khulna and Dhaka via Rajshahi and northern districts was restored on Sunday afternoon after the derailed compartments of an oil tanker train were recovered at the Sabdarpur railway station at Jhenaidah’s Kotchandpur upazila....

item.title: Bidi workers block highway in Kushtia for wage hike
item.description: BIDI workers in Kushtia’s Daulatpur upazila on Sunday blocked the Kushtia-Pragpur Highway, demanding an increase in wages and disrupting traffic for nearly an hour....

item.title: AL-JCD CLASH: 25 BCL, JL leaders arrested, OC closed
item.description: At least 25 leaders of Bangladesh Chhatra League, the banned student wing of the Awami League, and the youth wing, Juba League, have been arrested over Saturday’s clash between Awami League and the Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s student body Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, in Noakhali....

item.title: UIU secures 3rd place globally, 1st in Asia in URC
item.description: The UIU Mars Rover Team, operating under the Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics at United International University, has achieved a milestone by securing third place globally and first place in Asia for the fifth consecutive time at the University Rover Challenge 2026 in the United States recently....

item.title: DU celebrates Nawab Sir Salimullah’s birth anniv
item.description: The University of Dhaka celebrated the 155th anniversary of birth of Nawab Sir Salimullah on Sunday....

item.title: ‘Earth & Memory’ explores enduring human-landscape relations
item.description: Bangladeshi visual artist Biplab Biprodas’s third solo painting exhibition, titled ‘Earth & Memory’, explores the enduring relationship between humanity and rural landscape of Bangladesh, which is currently under way at the Alliance Française de Dhaka at Dhanmondi....

item.title: Greater fuel reserves, diverse imports urged
item.description: A special parliamentary committee recommended expanding Bangladesh’s strategic fuel reserves for at least three months, diversifying import sources, and introducing comprehensive automation and digital monitoring across the energy supply chain....

item.title: TIKA delegation calls on DU treasurer
item.description: A delegation from the Turkish Cooperation and Coordination Agency paid a courtesy call on Dhaka University treasurer Professor M Jahangir Alam Chowdhury at the office of the treasurer on Sunday....

item.title: Democratic Rights Committee wants trade deal with US scrapped
item.description: Leaders of the Democratic Rights Committee, Bangladesh on Sunday at a rally in Dhaka called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to scrap the unequal business deal signed with the United States during the interim government’s tenure, after discussing it in the current budget session....
